//_purpose: Copy to project.config.json. The (future) installer reads this to resolve tokens, render templates, and decide which overlays to apply. Until the CLI exists, use it as the substitution reference for a manual install.

project.name: My Project
project.//_profile_options: python-monolith | ts-frontend | ml-repo | infra-only | docs-only | minimal
project.//_roles_note: Core coordination roles ship with the harness. PROJECT-only specialists (e.g. a release role, an infra role) are added by the project in its own config; do not assume any here.
forbidden_patterns: // e.g. VPS_HOST_PATTERN for your demo infra, project JWT secret on CLI, project image build on VPS
project.//_product_prefixes_note: Directories that count as product code for coordination hints. Default for monorepos. coordination-hints-lib.js keeps this default in Phase 1; a future read from this field is documented as a TODO there.
project.//_ml_training_provider_options: none | <provider>. The harness core ships no ML-training provider integration. A project that needs one (GPU compose, a training-job skill, provider-specific custom verbs/hooks) adds it from its own overlay; the starter carries none.
project.//_compose_services_note: The project's actual service topology (app services, datastores, caches, object stores). The core ships NEUTRAL placeholders only; a Python monolith might list api/queue-consumer/web + a SQL store + a queue + an object store, a TS frontend might list web only. Fill these per project — the harness assumes none.
